title: Build deterministic Memory Drift checks in `hunch doctor` (dead-reference / doc-lies / contradiction)
context: This session surfaced real drift in Hunch's own memory: docs/veto.md, DESIGN.md and deep-synthesis.md all read 'proposed/no code yet' while the features had shipped, and a fresh decision (dec_1f5b265c80) contradicted roadmap #1. Decided after concluding that an AUTONOMOUS background cleanup/optimization agent is the wrong tool — an LLM auto-mutating the graph violates the model-out-of-the-block-path moat (see veto.md, dec_a466655539) and fails the recorded six-axis rubric on measurability + differentiation. Maintenance should be deterministic and human-gated. Roadmap item #7 in expansion-research.md.
decision: Add a deterministic (NO-LLM) drift dimension to `hunch doctor` (optionally CI). Three checks comparing existing records against the symbol/dep graph Hunch already builds: (1) DEAD-REFERENCE — a decision/constraint's related_files / related symbols / retired sets that no longer exist in src/ -> flag stale; (2) DOC-LIES — a doc whose status says 'proposed'/'not yet implemented' while the symbol/feature it names exists in the graph -> flag stale-status (the exact veto.md/DESIGN.md bug); (3) CONTRADICTION — two in-force decisions with overlapping scope and opposing tripwires/rules -> flag supersede candidate. Emit an ADVISORY drift report (mirrors the redundancy guard rendering). NEVER blocks, NEVER auto-fixes — a human reads it and confirms via the existing review/supersede flow. Reuses the symbols store + checkreport renderers; no new infra and no model in the loop.
consequences: Catches the exact drift this session found by hand; keeps docs and records honest as the graph grows.
Stays on the moat: deterministic, model-out-of-the-block-path, advisory-only.
Low effort — reuses the symbol/dep graph (indexer) + checkreport render path; pairs with roadmap #2 (eval harness) as quality infrastructure.
Doc-lies check is a light heuristic scan (status phrase + symbol-name presence) — advisory by nature, never a gate.
alternatives_rejected: Autonomous background cleanup/optimization AGENT — REJECTED: an LLM that auto-mutates the graph violates the model-out-of-the-block-path moat (veto.md, dec_a466655539), burns subscription tokens continuously, and fails the rubric's measurability + differentiation axes. The moat IS that no model sits in the loop.
Always-on daemon/watcher process — REJECTED: maintenance is deterministic and event-light; a scheduled hook / CI step or on-demand `hunch doctor` run covers it without a resident process.

Periodic LLM advisory reconciler (drafts -> review queue) — DEFERRED, not rejected: viable for SEMANTIC drift the deterministic checks can't judge, but only worth it once the graph is large; at 61 decisions solo the deterministic checks + occasional manual passes suffice.

Auto-fix / auto-supersede — REJECTED: writing or superseding records without human confirm breaks the human_confirmed provenance ladder (dec_57e5b6f700, dec_a466655539); drift checks advise, humans decide.
